methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Navier is a seed-stage transportation company that has raised $7.2M to date. The broader transportation sector contains five private peers at various stages, including Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, and REGENT Craft; no public comparables exist in the immediate peer set.

Given data constraints, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using total capital raised as a proxy base of $7M, application of a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to next-stage yields an implied valuation of $18M. This method is grounded in observed venture capital deployment patterns for early-stage transportation companies, though its applicability is inherently limited at seed stage where revenue and operational milestones remain nascent.

Peer-median triangulation cannot be applied meaningfully; the transportation sector peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables (zero same-stage, one adjacent-stage, zero similar-valuation-tier) to support reliable cross-sectional benchmarking. The stage-progression method therefore anchors the analysis alone. The resulting range is $15M (low) to $21M (high) around an $18M median, with the width reflecting typical early-stage valuation uncertainty. Key risks to this estimate include the nascent maturity of comparable exits in frontier transportation and the absence of revenue or unit economics data for calibration. Any revised valuation should incorporate subsequent fundraising signals or operational traction data.
